name: chart-bar-stacked
title: ChartBarStacked
description: Multi-series stacked bar chart for comparing raw totals or normalized 0–100% composition across ordered categories and time buckets.

intent.summary: Multi-series stacked bar chart for comparing raw totals or normalized 0–100% composition across ordered categories and time buckets.
useWhen: Users need to compare both a category total and the contribution of several consistent series.
A time series needs to show how its composition changes across equal time buckets.
Every category should normalize to 100% so proportional composition can be compared independently of total magnitude.
avoidWhen: Users need to compare individual series values precisely across categories; use grouped bars or separate charts.
The visualization has only one measure per category; use ChartBar.
The primary task is following continuous trends; use ChartLine.
Series contain negative values or do not align with the shared category list.

alternatives.when: Each category contains one independent value.
alternatives.reason: ChartBar provides the simpler single-series magnitude comparison.

alternatives.when: Users need to follow one or more trends rather than compare stack composition.
alternatives.reason: ChartLine preserves each series as a continuous path across the ordered axis.

alternatives.when: One category's parts-to-whole composition is the focus.
alternatives.reason: ChartDonut provides the dedicated single-total composition treatment.

accessibility: Provide a visible legend or adjacent textual summary that names every series; do not rely on segment color alone.
The chart image name includes each category and series value, using normalized percentages in percentage mode.
Use an adjacent data table when exact cross-category comparisons are necessary to complete the task.
states: Stacked mode preserves raw magnitudes and scales the y-axis to the largest category total.
Percentage mode normalizes each non-empty category independently and renders a fixed 0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, and 100% axis.
Series render bottom-to-top in authored order; keep that order and color mapping stable across categories.
Stacked mode gives only the top visible segment 2px top corners. Percentage mode keeps every corner square because the normalized stack touches both axis edges.
Adjacent segments are separated by a non-scaling 1px gap; the stack's outer edges do not receive that separator.
Negative values are unsupported and render as zero; the owning product supplies loading, empty, and error states.
responsiveBehavior: The SVG preserves its authored aspect ratio while shrinking to fit a narrower parent.
The parent supplies dimensions and chooses how many time buckets remain legible at each width.
Pre-format category labels and reduce bucket count before rendering when horizontal space becomes constrained.

customElements: Assign series and categories as JavaScript properties and keep every series aligned to the category count.
react: Pass stable series and category arrays; choose percentage variant only when each category represents a meaningful whole.
angular: Bind series and categories as properties; choose percentage variant only when each category represents a meaningful whole.

categories.description: X-axis labels — must match each series' `data` length. Set as a JS property.

series.description: Ordered stack series. Set as a JS property (not an HTML attribute).

categories.description: X-axis labels — must match each series' `data` length. Set as a JS property.

series.description: Ordered stack series. Set as a JS property (not an HTML attribute).

consumption.complexPropertyNote: Assign these non-primitive values as JavaScript properties: categories, series.

peerDependencies.frameworks: Custom Elements; React 18/19 wrappers; Angular 19-22 standalone adapters.
